rationale: The citing paper gives Van Heerde, Gupta, and Wittink (2003) a specific functional role in its literature positioning, summarizing its unit-sales decomposition and 33% brand-switching result as a key contrast to earlier higher switching claims. It then frames its own contribution as adding to this decomposition literature with a household-level dynamic structural model that allows flexible consumption and heterogeneity. The paper also interprets its findings as consistent with Van Heerde et al. (2003), using it as an empirical benchmark rather than a passing citation.
relation_description: The citing study extends the decomposition line of work represented by Van Heerde, Gupta, and Wittink (2003). It moves from that prior unit-sales/store-level evidence to a dynamic household-level structural framework and compares resulting component shares against the 2003 benchmark.
